Onam festival not only brings the mythical King Mahabali back to earth but also marks the harvest, while Vishu is the beginning of the Malayalam New Year. The common thing about these two celebrations is the festive food and it is the vast amount of food that these festivals symbolize which is the major reason for uniting people. The heritage of Kerala’s cuisine is greatly influenced by the traditional snacks that are made during these celebrations since they are prepared in the same ancient ways using the same local ingredients and sometimes even the same recipes as long ago.

Popular Traditional Kerala Snacks for Onam

Onam, in addition to being a festival, is synonymous with traditional Kerala food. During this ten-day celebration, besides the main course, the best and most loved Kerala snacks also come out. So, here they are:

  • Sharkara Varatti: One of the few snacks that have to be present at every traditional Onam feast. The mix of raw banana along with the sweet-and-spicy mixture results in this new and exciting crunchy delicacy.
  • Achappam (Rose Cookies): If there happens to be a dessert that could do an excellent job of serving as the evening tea partner it is achappam. In addition to being light, crispy, and mildly sweet achappam is also a Christmas treat.
  • Unniyappam : These little morsels are considered to be the best and the most fragrant food of Kerala, and they come from the frying of very ripe bananas, and the mixing of the same with sweetener and rice flour. Sometimes the soft and delicious fritters are called Unniyappam.
  • Kuzhalappam : This kind of snack is mainly so liked by the crispy family members due to the irresistible cylindrical shape, and the milk and cumin flavor that the snack is almost impossible to keep away from.

The making of these snacks is in huge quantities, and the distribution is such that they are first given to neighbors and then to guests which reflects the spirit of unity that Onam symbolizes.

Vishu Celebrations: the moment of traditional snacking 

The eye of the beholder on Vishu can’t help but be drawn to the mesmerizing Vishukkani and then the feasting and sweetmeats. The Vishu treats are less complicated, however, they are just as traditional as the Onam ones when in juxtaposition. 

  • Avalose Unda: This healthy and tasty snack is made by combining roasted rice flour, coconut, cumin seeds, and jaggery, and then making small balls of the mixture. 
  • Sukhiyan: A delightful snack that has a crispy outer layer and a soft center filled with ripe banana, covered in batter, and deep-fried is the one made by the filling of ripe banana stuffed into bread, turning in batter, and frying. 
  • Pazham Pori (Ethakka Appam): Another universal favorite of Vishu is the thick slices of Nendran bananas that are dipped in batter and fried until golden, a classic Vishu’s favorite. 
  • The mixtures and banana chips :  They are inseparable from the visuals of Kerala festivals—the hot and spicy mixture and crisp banana chips come hand in hand, all fried in pure coconut oil, to give the mouthwatering taste. 

The snacks do not only speak of taste but also speak of hope, prosperity, and new beginnings.

The Importance of Traditional Ingredients

The traditional and commonly used ingredients such as coconut oil, jaggery, rice flour, spices, and certain types of local bananas not only characterize but also rank Kerala’s snacks in the quality and taste scale as compared to others. The use of the age-old methods of food preparation, on the other hand, guarantees the retention of the original flavor, fragrance, and nutrients, thus the snack is regarded as the embodiment of the creative and varied culinary expertise of Karnataka. 

Keeping Traditions Alive in Modern Times

The modern family lives have become so fast that they are relying more on those brands that keep the quality while preserving the authenticity. Through the snacks made with traditional recipes, modern households still celebrate festive occasions the way the culture dictates.

Pavithram is the supplier of a range of cooking oils, traditional kerala snacks and spices that are genuine and bring the spirit of Kerala’s culinary legacy to every house. Each of Pavithram’s products that are based on purity, freshness, and old-style craftsmanship, manifests the opulence of Kerala’s culture and tastes. Pavithram provides extra virgin oils that are full of nutrients and can increase the taste of daily meals, and snacks that are made by time-honoured techniques, besides that, there are spices that are genuine and come from trustworthy farms, which means that they offer quality that is never compromised. Each product is meant to give you the taste, the smell, and the authenticity having the link to the true culinary roots of Kerala.

Celebrating Onam and Vishu with real traditional Kerala snacks is not only an eating experience but also a way of giving respect to the heritage, culture, and the everlasting tastes that have been handed down through generations.

title: Health Benefits of Common Kerala Spices You Can Buy Online

content:
Kerala, frequently referred to as the “Land of Spices”, has been a center for the production of fragrant and medicinal spices for the world for many centuries. Spices from Kerala are not only known for their strong flavors but also their use in Ayurveda and traditional health practices. However, today, online shopping offers you the luxury to place an order for these strong spices with just a click, hence, allowing you to reap the taste and health benefits simultaneously at the comfort of your home. We are going to discover the few typical Kerala spices that can be purchased through the internet and their role in promoting health.

Black Pepper (Kurumulaku) – The Spice King

The black pepper of Kerala is among the most amazing spices and is also the first one in the list of spices in Indian cuisine. It is loaded with piperine which is responsible for the digestion, metabolism, and nutrient absorption of the body being enhanced. Besides, black pepper gets acknowledgment for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties and thus can support the immune system and alleviate or lessen the symptoms of cold and cough.

Turmeric (Manjal) – The Healing Spice of Nature

Turmeric is a herb that has been considered very useful for health since its discovery. An active ingredient in turmeric known as Curcumin is responsible for the strongest anti-inflammatory and antibacterial actions among the others. To use turmeric in the diet is almost equal to the prevention of arthritis,  to the improvement of skin conditions, and to the strengthening of the immune system. In Kerala, turmeric has not only been the most beneficial spice in cooking but also the primary ingredient in traditional cures and beauty treatments.

Cardamom (Elakka) – The Queen of Spices

Cardamom’s presence in a dish and drink is very noticeable as it contributes a sweet and cooling smell. Its’ benefits to the body include aiding digestion, reducing acidity, and promoting oral health. Besides, cardamom is said to be the best when it comes to enhancing lung capacity and purifying the system. A cup of hot tea with cardamom is indeed a very nice way of unwinding and having one’s brain free of mental fog.

Cinnamon (Karuvapatta) – A Natural Metabolism Booster

Cinnamon has been a great spice throughout history for sweetening up any savory dish, and besides that, it has the ability to regulate blood sugar levels and increase insulin sensitivity. It is very rich in antioxidants and, at the same time, it has an ability to reduce inflammation. If taken every day, it could improve heart health and help maintain weight which is the reason why a lot of people concerned about their health, use it.

Cloves (Grambu) – Little Spice, Huge Benefits

Cloves are an extremely powerful medicine box, and their main applications are in dental and stomach health. Their analgesic, digestive, and disinfectant properties make them capable of fighting and relieving infections, respectively. Besides, among the various applications of clove oil, one of them in modern herbalist’s practice is that it serves as a pain and inflammation reliever.

Ginger (Inji) – The Root of Daily Health

Ginger is widely used in cooking and medicinal practices in Kerala. The primary purposes of ginger are to alleviate vomiting, improve the digestive system’s operations, and increase the body’s resistance to infections. Ginger’s anti-inflammatory action has also permitted its use as a soothing agent in muscle pain treatment and as a preventive measure in the case of respiratory tract diseases.

Fenugreek (Uluva) – A Marvel of Nutrients

Fenugreek seeds are not just a high source of fiber but also pack a good amount of vitamins and minerals. The main health-related effects of the herb include controlling blood sugar, enhancing digestion, and boosting heart health. Kerala has been using fenugreek for body health maintenance both in curries and as a part of herbal medicines.

Reasons for Buying Kerala Spices Through Online Means?

The online purchase of Kerala spices guarantees the sale of fresh, genuine, and farm-sourced products. Trustworthy online shops not only offer the taste of the traditional beloved flavors but also enable the consumer to get the health benefits of these spices which have been nature’s gifts. Good packing along with quality control work together in online shopping to retain the aroma, taste, and nutritional value.

From vitamins and minerals packed oils that improve your daily food to traditional Kerala snacks prepared using time-tested methods and real Kerala spices from trustworthy farms, Pavithram assures that the quality can never be compromised. title: Best Cooking Oil Good for Health: A Complete Guide to Choose the Right One
excerpt: Selecting the healthiest cooking oil has turned out to be a crucial factor for a wholesome lifestyle. Nowadays, the opti...
content:
Selecting the healthiest cooking oil has turned out to be a crucial factor for a wholesome lifestyle. Nowadays, the options are plentiful and range from cold-pressed oils, refined oils, seed oils, to traditional Indian options which may lead to confusion about what really is your health’s best friend. The appropriate cooking oil, besides its flavour-enhancing property, is claimed to be beneficial for heart health, anti-inflammatory, and even nutrient supplying.

Through our blog, we are going to uncover the healthiest oil types for cooking, their pros, and how you can come up with the best one regarding your daily meals.

A different kind of oil to a different kind of cooking process 

By Indian cooking, oil is one of the most significant ingredients that on the whole in the flavor of the dish very much. Different types of oils for various purposes like sautéing and frying, and of course tempering, influence the taste and nutrition of the dish besides the amount of oil used. Nevertheless, using an inferior oil even once can possibly result to very serious health problems such as the rise in bad cholesterol, overweight, and even cancer in the long run. Superior quality cooking oil should have the following: 

  • An ideal ratio of omega-3 to omega-6 fatty acids.
  • A smoking point that is sufficiently high to go along with your cooking method 
  • No destructive chemicals or additives 
  • All antioxidants and vitamins are in the natural forms and are present

Top Healthy Cooking Oils You Can Choose

  1. Coconut Oil

In the southern part of India and particularly in Kerala, coconut oil is the number one cooking oil. It has an extremely high amount of MCTs (Medium-Chain Triglycerides), which are not only speeding up the metabolism but also supporting the heart.

  1. Olive Oil

Extra virgin olive oil is one of the best and healthiest fats for the heart that has a very high score on the health scale mainly because of the presence of mono-unsaturated fats and polyphenols. It aids in lowering the body’s inflammatory reaction and thus extending the lifespan of the heart and blood vessels. It is suggested for salads, grilling, and cooking at low temperatures.

  1. Sesame Oil

Sesame oil, along with the traditional oils, is among the healthiest oils and this is mainly due to its content of strong antioxidants like sesamol and sesamin in large quantities. It enhances the immune system of the body, improves the functioning of the intestines, and is good for frying and making traditional dishes.

  1. Groundnut Oil

Groundnut oil is very rich in monounsaturated fats and thus has a high smoke point which makes it suitable for Indian deep-frying. It is good for heart and brings a very subtle nutty taste.

  1. Mustard Oil

Mustard oil is very rich in omega-3 fatty acids and has got the natural antibacterial properties as well. It increases the level of good cholesterol, aids digestion and has a very strong scent and health benefits which is why East and North Indian households rely heavily on it.

  1. Sunflower Oil

On the other hand, sunflower oil has an outstanding content of vitamin E and unsaturated fatty acids. These substances are good for the skin and the heart but the oil with high omega-6 requires caution in consumption. This oil can be used for daily cooking and frying.

How to Select the Ideal Cooking Oil for Your Kitchen

Different oils have their specific benefits but the most appropriate way of using them is by changing them according to your cooking needs which assures you a varied intake of nutrients and flavors.

Let me tell you what to choose. 

  • Out of lipid content, go for cold-pressed oils.
  • High-temperature frying? Then you require oils with high-temperature resistance.
  • Prefer the oils that are produced by trustworthy brands who offer their purity and freshness as a guarantee.
  • Do not use oils that have been extremely refined, because these are the ones that might still have chemicals or solvents having been used in their production.

With a well-balanced mix of coconut oil, sesame oil, groundnut oil, and olive oil, it will certainly be possible for you to not only maintain good health but also have tasty meals.

Final Thoughts

It is up to you to decide which cooking oil is the best among the ones that are the most nutritious according to your case and at the same time go along with your cooking style and taste preferences. By the way of using pure, cold-pressed, and traditionally produced oils, your health and your food’s authenticity can be improved together.

title: 7 Proven Healthy Ways to Use Coconut Oil and Sesame Oil in Everyday Cooking
excerpt: Cooking oil is more than just a kitchen essential, it’s the heart of every meal. The oil you choose determines not just ...
content:
Cooking oil is more than just a kitchen essential, it’s the heart of every meal. The oil you choose determines not just the taste of your dishes but also your long-term health. From sautéing and frying to adding a glossy finish to your food, the right oil can turn an ordinary meal into something wholesome and flavourful.

At Pavithram Oil, we understand that choosing a healthy oil is about finding balance, between tradition and nutrition, flavour and function. Among the most widely used and trusted oils in Indian kitchens are coconut oil and sesame oil, each with its unique properties, taste, and benefits.

But even the best oils can lose their value if not used correctly. That’s why we’ve put together 7 proven, healthy, and science-backed ways to use coconut and sesame oil inspired by recommendations from the World Health Organization (WHO) and leading nutrition experts.

1. Use Coconut Oil in the Right Quantity

Coconut oil is a beloved ingredient in Indian homes, especially in Kerala. Its aroma and flavour add depth to traditional dishes, and it’s a rich source of natural fats that support energy.
 However, it’s important to remember that coconut oil is high in saturated fats, about 80–90% of its composition. According to WHO guidelines, saturated fats should make up no more than 10% of your total daily energy intake.

That doesn’t mean you should avoid coconut oil, it means you should use it in moderation. A spoonful or two for everyday cooking is sufficient for most households.
 Use it where it matters most:

  • For tempering or sautéing vegetables
  • In traditional curries and stir-fries
  • As a flavourful base for regional dishes

Moderate use allows you to enjoy the traditional goodness of coconut oil while keeping your fat intake within healthy limits.

2. Balance with Unsaturated Oils Like Sesame Oil

Health experts around the world agree that unsaturated fats are heart-friendly fats. Unlike saturated fats, they help reduce LDL (bad cholesterol) and may increase HDL (good cholesterol).

This is where sesame oil shines. It’s naturally rich in monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fatty acids, both of which promote cardiovascular health when used in place of saturated fats. Sesame oil also contains vitamin E and natural antioxidants like sesamin and sesamol, known for their anti-inflammatory properties.

Here’s a simple way to bring balance to your diet:

  • Use Pavithram Coconut Oil for traditional dishes and high-flavour recipes.
  • Use Pavithram Sesame Oil for daily cooking, stir-frying, and as a light finishing oil.

By alternating between the two, you can enjoy a mix of fats that supports both flavour and health just as WHO recommends.

3. Avoid Overheating Oils

Every oil has a smoke point, the temperature at which it starts to break down and release harmful compounds. Overheating oil not only affects its taste but can also destroy beneficial nutrients and form free radicals.

  • Refined coconut oil has a smoke point of about 230°C, which makes it suitable for frying, roasting, and everyday Indian cooking.
  • Refined sesame oil also has a high smoke point, making it a great option for deep-frying or high-heat stir-frying.

When cooking, try to keep your flame medium. Avoid letting oil smoke or darken. For health and taste, it’s always better to heat oil gradually and avoid burning it.

4. Store Oils Correctly to Maintain Freshness

Oil quality can deteriorate quickly if not stored properly. Light, heat, and air exposure can cause oils to oxidize, leading to a stale smell, loss of flavour, and even harmful compounds.

To preserve the natural quality of your Pavithram oils:

  • Store them in airtight containers.
  • Keep bottles in a cool, dark cupboard away from direct sunlight or the stove.
  • Avoid transferring oil into open or metal containers that may react with it.
  • Buy oils in smaller quantities if you don’t use them quickly.

Proper storage helps your oil stay fresh longer and retain its nutritional benefits.

5. Don’t Reuse Oil Repeatedly

It’s a common habit to reuse oil after frying, especially in Indian households. However, doing this too often can be harmful. Reheating oil multiple times changes its structure, leading to the formation of trans fats and oxidized compounds that are harmful to heart and liver health.

WHO and global food safety organizations advise not to reuse oil more than once, especially for deep frying. If you must reuse oil, make sure to:

  • Filter out any food particles after frying.
  • Store it in a clean, airtight container.
  • Use it for low-heat cooking the next day, not for deep frying again.

Remember: fresh oil equals fresh health.

6. Mix and Match for a Balanced Diet

No single oil provides all the essential fatty acids your body needs. The key to healthy cooking lies in mixing and rotating oils.
 Using both coconut and sesame oils allows you to benefit from two unique profiles:

  • Coconut oil provides flavour and medium-chain triglycerides (MCTs) that support quick energy metabolism.
  • Sesame oil provides unsaturated fats and antioxidants that promote heart health.

A balanced combination of the two ensures your body gets both stability from saturated fats and flexibility from unsaturated fats.

For instance:

  • Use coconut oil for traditional curries, seafood dishes, and sweets.
  • Use sesame oil for salads, tandoor-style cooking, or to enhance South Indian delicacies like idli podi or pickles.

This natural balance aligns perfectly with WHO’s advice  more unsaturated fats, less trans and saturated fats.

7. Choose Purity and Quality Over Quantity

One of the most important rules for healthy cooking is to choose pure, high-quality oils from trusted sources. Many cheaper alternatives may be blended or contain additives that affect quality.

Pavithram Oils are refined and processed with care to maintain purity and consistency free from impurities and artificial colours. Each batch is tested to ensure the oil retains its natural nutritional properties and is safe for everyday cooking.

High-quality oils also ensure that your dishes:

  • Absorb less oil during frying
  • Stay fresh longer
  • Taste authentic, just as they should

With Pavithram, you can be sure that every drop you pour into your pan is pure, trusted, and made for your family’s health.

Bonus: How to Use Coconut and Sesame Oils Smartly in Daily Life

Here are some additional healthy practices for everyday cooking with Pavithram Oils:

  1. Measure your oil intake – Use a teaspoon instead of pouring directly from the bottle.
  2. Combine oils wisely – You can mix coconut and sesame oil in a 1:1 ratio for a balanced flavour and nutritional blend.
  3. Include more steamed and grilled dishes – Use oils minimally but effectively for taste.
  4. Focus on variety – Along with these oils, occasionally include other sources of healthy fats like nuts, seeds, or fish.
  5. Maintain a clean cooking setup – Avoid leaving oil residues on the stove; cleanliness extends the oil’s freshness.

These simple steps make your meals not just tasty but also health-focused — the way cooking should be.

A Quick Look: Coconut Oil vs. Sesame Oil

Feature Coconut Oil Sesame Oil
Type of Fat Mostly Saturated Mostly Unsaturated
Smoke Point (Refined) ~230°C ~230°C
Ideal For Deep frying, curries, traditional Kerala dishes Stir-frying, sautéing, salads, pickles
Health Benefits Provides energy, supports metabolism Heart-healthy fats, antioxidant-rich
Flavour Rich, aromatic Mild, nutty

Both oils are excellent when used correctly, it’s about how and how much you use.

The Pavithram Way to Healthier Cooking

At Pavithram, we believe that the true flavour of food comes from purity. Our refined coconut oil and refined sesame oil are crafted to preserve the authentic aroma and nutritional benefits of nature while meeting modern quality standards.

We source the finest raw materials and use precision refining to deliver oils that:

  • Stay light and fresh
  • Retain their natural taste
  • Are ideal for all types of cooking

Every bottle of Pavithram stands for purity, trust, and tradition, helping families cook the healthy way every day.
